_meta.version_note: v1.1 patch (S53): C-DRP.8 (UMP) added per BLOCKER-2 resolution, R-13. All other claims and structure unchanged from v1.

paper_title: Dual-Reader Scientific Publishing: A Framework for Machine-Verifiable Knowledge Corpora

phase_ready.note: I-9 run S47. Fixed: _meta added, paper_title added, version v0.3->v1, predicate field names uppercase per schema, 3 unregistered prose claims registered (C-DRP.3 Type D, C-DRP.4 Type D, C-DRP.5 Type F), C-DRP.1 evaluation rewritten to remove NL judgment. Prior C-DRP.3 and C-DRP.4 renumbered C-DRP.6 and C-DRP.7. v1.1 (S53): C-DRP.8 (UMP theorem) added. Claim was proved in DRP-1 v1.1 prose and planned in S47 renumbering but not executed. ARCH-2 and DRP-2 prose references to UMP must now cite C-DRP.8, not C-DRP.7.

claim_registry.label: Syntax Necessity and Sufficiency
claim_registry.statement: The 5-part predicate syntax (FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT) is necessary and sufficient to express any falsifiable scientific claim as a machine-evaluable predicate.
claim_registry.scope_note_S58: Grok QC (confidence 0.75) notes scope mismatch: statement claims universality ('any falsifiable scientific claim') but predicate tests only against the Fracttalix corpus. Universality is unfalsifiable within any finite corpus. Recommend narrowing statement to 'any falsifiable claim expressible as a finite deterministic evaluation' or expanding test set to include external claims.

WHERE.n_inexpressible: count of falsifiable claims in the Fracttalix corpus for which no 5-part predicate can be constructed that satisfies all schema validation constraints (FALSIFIED_IF: named variables only; WHERE: typed definitions; EVALUATION: finite terminating procedure; BOUNDARY: explicit threshold case; CONTEXT: threshold justification)

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each falsifiable claim in claim_registry across all corpus AI layers: (1) load existing predicate if present; (2) run schema validator against predicate object; (3) if validator returns errors OR predicate absent: increment n_inexpressible; (4) report count. Tool: JSON schema validator against ai-layer-schema.json. Procedure terminates in finite steps bounded by claim count.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP.1 · threshold 0 · necessity claim: any single schema-failing predicate falsifies sufficiency; any claim requiring a 6th field falsifies necessity · evaluability is determined by schema validation, not human judgment

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: A claim in the corpus for which schema validator rejects all attempted 5-part predicate formulations.

claim_registry.label: Gate Enforcement Requirement
claim_registry.statement: Gate enforcement — not format alone — is required for sustained DRS adoption: without a phase gate, the AI layer will be omitted in the majority of papers in any corpus claiming to use the DRS.

WHERE.n_omitted_optional: count of papers in a corpus that declares DRS adoption as optional (no phase gate) and that have been published without a schema-valid AI layer
WHERE.n_total_papers: total count of papers published in that same corpus (minimum 10)

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Identify a corpus that declares DRS format as optional (no phase gate blocking publication) with n_total_papers >= 10; (2) count published papers lacking a schema-valid AI layer (n_omitted_optional); (3) count total published papers (n_total_papers); (4) evaluate: n_omitted_optional <= 0.5 * n_total_papers. Finite deterministic audit.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: fraction = 0.5 -> FALSIFIED (inclusive); fraction > 0.5 -> NOT FALSIFIED (majority did omit, consistent with claim)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP.2 · threshold 0.5 · claim predicts majority omission without gate enforcement · FALSIFIED if omission rate is at or below 50% (i.e., most papers included AI layers voluntarily, contradicting the claim) · minimum corpus size 10 to avoid small-sample noise · S58 correction: predicate logic was inverted in prior version (flagged by Grok QC review)

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: A corpus of 20 papers declaring optional DRS in which only 8 are published without AI layers (40% omission — below majority threshold).

claim_registry.label: DRS Self-Sufficiency Definition
claim_registry.statement: The DRS enables any AI system with access to the AI layer to verify any falsifiable claim in the corpus without reading the prose narrative — self-sufficiency is a design requirement, not an empirical claim, and is enforced by C5 at every PHASE-READY gate.

claim_registry.label: DRS Core Requirement Definition
claim_registry.statement: In a DRS corpus, every falsifiable claim is associated with a machine-evaluable falsification predicate satisfying the 5-part syntax; this association is mandatory, not optional, and is enforced by the PHASE-READY gate.

claim_registry.label: Live C5 Instance — MK-P1
claim_registry.statement: The MK-P1 AI layer achieves C5 self-sufficiency: an AI system can verify claim F-MK1.1 using only the structured layer, requiring zero prose references.

WHERE.n_prose_refs_MKP1: count of prose references required to complete the verification trace for claim F-MK1.1 as recorded in the channel_2_query_trace field of MK-P1-ai-layer.json

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Load MK-P1-ai-layer.json from github.com/thomasbrennan/Fracttalix/ai-layers/MK-P1-ai-layer.json; (2) locate channel_2_query_trace field; (3) count any step in the trace that requires loading or reading prose content (n_prose_refs_MKP1); (4) evaluate condition. Finite deterministic procedure.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP.5 · threshold 0 · zero-prose criterion · query trace is the committed record in MK-P1-ai-layer.json — evaluation references that file, not the prose paper

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: The query trace for F-MK1.1 contains a step requiring prose content to obtain a formula or value.

claim_registry.label: PHASE-READY Definition
claim_registry.statement: PHASE-READY: AI layer satisfies C1 (schema valid), C2 (all falsifiable claims registered with predicates), C3 (all predicates machine-evaluable), C5 (verification self-sufficient), C6 (all predicates non-vacuous); C4 tracked in placeholder register, non-blocking.

claim_registry.label: CORPUS-COMPLETE Definition
claim_registry.statement: CORPUS-COMPLETE: all papers PHASE-READY, C4 (dependency closure) fully satisfied with zero unresolved placeholders across all corpus objects.

claim_registry.label: Upstream Measurement Principle (UMP)
claim_registry.statement: For any falsification predicate K = (P, O, M, B), the causal independence of O from P is a necessary condition for non-vacuity. This is a theorem proved by contradiction in DRP-1 v1.1 §10.7, not a methodological convention. A predicate where O is derived from or conditioned on P is structurally vacuous regardless of the contents of M or B: no measurement procedure can extract information about P from observations defined relative to P.
claim_registry.addition_reason: UMP proved in DRP-1 v1.1 prose but absent from v1 AI layer. Registered per BLOCKER-2 resolution (R-13). Claim ID C-DRP.8 consistent with S47 renumbering plan recorded in phase_ready note.
claim_registry.note: UMP is a theorem (type D). Received as Type A axiom in DRP-2 (C-DRP2.5). DRP-3 receives C-DRP2.4 from DRP-2, which is itself derived from the UMP — C-DRP.8 is therefore an indirect ancestor of DRP-3 but is not a direct inbound edge in DRP-3's AI layer.

placeholder_register.description: Section 7.4 live demonstrated instance — first paper PHASE-READY with published AI layer
